finding.summary: Tonal balance is excessively dark; strong low end at 100 Hz with significant roll-off above, making the clean guitar sound muddy and lacking presence.
finding.probable_cause: The recorded source likely has limited high-frequency content, and no EQ is applied to brighten the tone.
finding.confidence_reason: The spectrum shows a clear 6 dB drop per octave upward from 100 Hz to 4 kHz, which is consistent with a dark tone; silence fraction is low so measurements are reliable.

evidence_refs.description: 1 kHz band at -24 dBFS (6 dB below 100 Hz)
4 kHz band at -30 dBFS (12 dB below 100 Hz)
Crest factor 12 dB indicates moderate dynamics, not overcompressed

proposal.reason: No EQ or other tonal-shaping FX in the chain; the only supported operations (volume, pan) cannot address the spectral imbalance.
